goal: Implement Pokegear — Clock card, Map card (Johto/Kanto toggle, fly destinations), Phone card (10 contacts, call mechanics)
abstract_tasks: Implement Pokegear 13-state machine (CLOCKINIT/CLOCKJOYPAD, MAPCHECKREGION, JOHTOMAPINIT/JOYPAD, KANTOMAPINIT/JOYPAD, PHONEINIT/JOYPAD/MAKEPHONECALL/FINISHPHONECALL, RADIOINIT/JOYPAD) with Left/Right card cycling
Implement Clock card: display current RTC time (HH:MM) and day-of-week; define morning/day/night period boundaries (4AM-9:59AM, 10AM-5:59PM, 6PM-3:59AM)
Implement Map card: Johto and Kanto region displays toggled with Select; animated player-position icon; Fast Ship icon on S.S. Aqua; cursor landmark naming
Implement Fly destination display on Map card — only landmarks visited by player are valid targets; enumerate all 12 Johto and 11 Kanto fly points per pokecrystal data
Implement Phone card UI: scrollable contact list (up to 10 slots), D-pad navigation, A to initiate call, B to close; permanent contacts (Mom, Prof. Elm) cannot be deleted
Implement card availability gating: Map card unlocked by Guide Gent in Cherrygrove; Radio card unlocked after Radio Tower quiz; EXPN Card adds Kanto stations
Implement Pokegear music handling: preserve map music on open; restore map music (RESTART_MAP_MUSIC=$FE) or use enter-map transition (ENTER_MAP_MUSIC=$FF) on close

source_data.pokegear.md:
## Cards
### 1. Clock Card (POKEGEARCARD_CLOCK)
Available: From game start
Time periods:
- Morning: 4:00 AM - 9:59 AM
- Day: 10:00 AM - 5:59 PM
- Night: 6:00 PM - 3:59 AM

### 2. Map Card (POKEGEARCARD_MAP)
Available: Given by the Guide Gent in Cherrygrove City (first visit)
Features:
- Cursor to any landmark to see its name
- Player icon shows current position (animated walking sprite)
- When on the S.S. Aqua, a special Fast Ship icon replaces the player sprite
- Shows Fly destinations (landmarks only, not routes)
- Two regions: JOHTO_REGION and KANTO_REGION, switchable with Select

### 3. Phone Card (POKEGEARCARD_PHONE)
UI: Scrollable list of up to 10 contacts. Cursor navigates with D-pad, A to call, B to close.

### 4. Radio Card (POKEGEARCARD_RADIO)
Available: Obtained from the Goldenrod Radio Tower after answering 5 quiz questions correctly
Upgrades: EXPN Card from Lavender Radio Tower manager.

## State Machine
13 states: CLOCKINIT/CLOCKJOYPAD, MAPCHECKREGION/JOHTOMAPINIT/JOHTOMAPJOYPAD, KANTOMAPINIT/KANTOMAPJOYPAD, PHONEINIT/PHONEJOYPAD/MAKEPHONECALL/FINISHPHONECALL, RADIOINIT/RADIOJOYPAD

## Fly System
### Johto Fly Points
New Bark Town, Cherrygrove City, Violet City, Azalea Town, Goldenrod City, Ecruteak City, Olivine City, Cianwood City, Mahogany Town, Lake of Rage, Blackthorn City, Silver Cave
### Kanto Fly Points
Pallet Town, Viridian City, Pewter City, Cerulean City, Vermilion City, Celadon City, Lavender Town, Saffron City, Fuchsia City, Cinnabar Island, Indigo Plateau
You can only Fly to cities/landmarks you have visited.

unstructured_notes: The Pokegear is given by Mom at game start. Clock and Phone cards are immediately available; Map card requires Guide Gent event in Cherrygrove. Radio card requires successfully answering 5 quiz questions at Goldenrod Radio Tower 1F. The state machine transitions drive the UI -- each card has its own INIT and JOYPAD state pair. Left/Right on D-pad cycles through unlocked cards only. The indicator arrow at top shows current card position. Map card shows player as an animated walking sprite; S.S. Aqua replaces this with a ship icon. Fly destination validity is per-visited-landmark, not all landmarks. Music must be restored correctly on exit depending on whether a radio station was playing.

goal: Implement phone system — call scheduling, incoming calls (hints, rematch offers, item gifts), trainer contact registration, call text content
abstract_tasks: Implement phone contact struct (PHONE_CONTACT_SIZE): trainer class/ID, two script bank/addr/time pairs (player-initiated and NPC-initiated), map group/number fields
Implement AddPhoneNumber, DelCellNum, CheckCellNum, GetRemainingSpaceInPhoneList; enforce CONTACT_LIST_SIZE = 10 cap (permanent contacts excluded from count)

Implement CheckPhoneCall trigger pipeline: (1) not on entrance tile, (2) receive-call timer elapsed, (3) 50% RNG check, (4) map phone service enabled, (5) GetAvailableCallers > 0; select caller via Random swap+mask mod count
Implement call delay scheduling: wTimeCyclesSinceLastCall cycles 0-3 with delays 20/10/5/3 minutes; reset after each successful call
Implement RingTwice_StartCall presentation: play SFX_CALL, show caller name textbox with 2 ring cycles, execute NPC-initiated Script2, waitbutton, HangUp (SFX_HANG_UP + boop animation)
Implement outgoing call validation from Pokegear: not in link mode, map has phone service, SCRIPT1_TIME matches time-of-day, same-map check ('Just go talk to them!'), out-of-area fallback

Implement all 30 trainer contact scripts covering: rematch requests with landmark name, Pokemon sightings, training updates, item gifts (Beverly=Nugget, Jose=StarPiece, Gina=LeafStone, Alan=FireStone, Liz=Thunderstone, Tully=WaterStone, Tiffany=PinkBow), swarm reports (Ralph=Qwilfish, Arnie=Yanma, Wilton=Remoraid), gossip (Brent/Chad/Irwin/Jack/Liz)

Implement Special Phone Call system: 8 Elm story calls (SPECIALCALL_POKERUS, SPECIALCALL_ROBBED, SPECIALCALL_ASSISTANT, SPECIALCALL_WEIRDBROADCAST, SPECIALCALL_SSTICKET, SPECIALCALL_BIKESHOP, SPECIALCALL_WORRIED, SPECIALCALL_MASTERBALL); Mom savings management calls; Bill PC-box-full call

source_data.phone_engine.md:
### Phone Contact Struct (PHONE_CONTACT_SIZE bytes)
| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| PHONE_CONTACT_TRAINER_CLASS | Trainer class (0 for non-trainer) |
| PHONE_CONTACT_TRAINER_ID | Trainer ID within class |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_BANK | Bank for player-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_ADDR | Address for player-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_TIME | Time-of-day mask for player calls |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_BANK | Bank for NPC-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_ADDR | Address for NPC-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_TIME | Time-of-day mask for incoming calls |
| PHONE_CONTACT_MAP_GROUP | Map group where contact resides |
| PHONE_CONTACT_MAP_NUMBER | Map number where contact resides |

### Call Timing
- Cycle 0: 20 minutes, Cycle 1: 10 minutes, Cycle 2: 5 minutes, Cycle 3+: 3 minutes

### Rematch System
Per-caller ENGINE flags: ENGINE_{NAME}_READY_FOR_REMATCH, ENGINE_{NAME}_{DAY}_{TIME}

### Item Gift System
Male: Jose, Wade, Alan, Derek, Tully, Wilton
Female: Beverly, Gina, Dana, Tiffany
wDailyPhoneItemFlags tracks per-caller gifts
source_data.phone_call_content.md:
## Phone Call Types
#### 1. Rematch Requests
Joey: 'Let's get together and battle! I promise things will be different! [Route 30] is where I'll be.'
#### 4. Item Gifts
Beverly: Nugget, Jose: Star Piece, Wade: Various, Gina: Leaf Stone, Alan: Fire Stone, Liz: Thunderstone, Tully: Water Stone, Tiffany: Pink Bow
#### 5. Swarm Reports
Ralph (Route 32): Qwilfish, Arnie (Route 35): Yanma, Wilton (Route 44): Remoraid

## Special Phone Calls
| # | Caller | Condition | Script |
|---|--------|-----------|--------|
| 1-4 | Prof. Elm | Only when outside | Various story updates |
| 5 | Prof. Elm | Wherever you are | Urgent story call |
| 6 | Bike Shop | Wherever you are | Bike is ready |
| 7 | Mom | Wherever you are | Lecture about saving money |
| 8 | Prof. Elm | Only when outside | Story update |

unstructured_notes: The phone system has 30 trainer contacts (28 in the numbered table plus Jack and Beverly at National Park). All trainer contacts can call ANYTIME time-of-day. Daily phone item flags (wDailyPhoneItemFlags) track per-caller item gifts -- they reset daily. Swarm reports override normal encounter tables until midnight. Irwin is notorious for calling frequently. The generic call flow uses RandomPhoneMon, RandomPhoneWildMon, and branches on 50% for brag vs generic content. Joey uniquely has a 2/3 chance to request a battle. Mom's savings management is a separate system tied to her SCRIPT2 calls. BrokenPlaceFarString bug exists in unused 'phonecall' script command -- do not implement that path. Wrong-number-on-empty-slot behavior (wPhoneList zero entry) must display WrongNumber script.

goal: QA audit of sprints 199-200 — verify Pokegear card unlocking, map display, phone contact list, call scheduling, rematch triggers
abstract_tasks: Verify Pokegear card availability gating: Clock and Phone available from start, Map card only after Cherrygrove Guide Gent event, Radio card only after Goldenrod Radio Tower quiz
Verify Map card region toggle (Johto/Kanto via Select), fly-point visibility restricted to visited landmarks only, S.S. Aqua ship icon appears correctly
Verify phone contact list capacity: exactly 10 slots, permanent contacts (Mom, Elm) not counting against limit, correct display of trainer class+name format vs non-trainer name format
Verify call trigger pipeline: all 5 conditions must be met simultaneously; test that any single condition failure suppresses the call
Verify call delay cycle timing: cycle 0=20min, 1=10min, 2=5min, 3+=3min; confirm reset after each call and cycle increment logic
Verify incoming call presentation sequence: SFX_CALL -> caller name 2-ring textbox -> Script2 execution -> waitbutton -> SFX_HANG_UP; no calls in gyms/dungeons with phone_flag=TRUE
Verify trainer rematch flag system: ENGINE_{NAME}_READY_FOR_REMATCH set/cleared correctly, daily flags reset by CheckDailyResetTimer, item gift flags (wDailyPhoneItemFlags) reset daily
Verify all 8 Special Phone Calls trigger at correct story moments and respect location constraints (outside-only vs anywhere)

source_data.pokegear.md:
### Call Trigger Conditions
All must be true:
1. Player not standing on entrance tile
2. Receive call timer has elapsed
3. 50% random chance
4. Map has phone service (phone_flag = FALSE)
5. At least one registered contact available

### Permanent Contacts
| Contact | When Registered |
|---------|----------------|
| Mom | Always registered from start |
| Prof. Elm | Registered from start |
| Prof. Oak | After getting the Radio Card |
| Bill | After meeting Bill |

### Trainer Swarm System
| Reporter | Route | Swarm Pokemon |
|----------|-------|---------------|
| Fisher Ralph | Route 32 | Qwilfish |
| Hiker Anthony | Route 33 | Dunsparce |
| Bug Catcher Arnie | Route 35 | Yanma |
| Fisher Wilton | Route 44 | Remoraid |
| Hiker Parry | Route 45 | Marill |
source_data.phone_engine.md:
### Known Bugs
1. BrokenPlaceFarString: PhoneCall routine calls BrokenPlaceFarString not in bank 0 -- crash if called
2. Wrong number on empty slot: If wPhoneList has a zero entry selected, displays WrongNumber script

### Key RAM Variables
| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| wPhoneList | Contact ID array |
| wTimeCyclesSinceLastCall | Call delay cycle counter (0-3) |
| wReceiveCallDelay_MinsRemaining | Minutes until next call eligible |
| wNumAvailableCallers | Count of eligible callers |
| wDailyPhoneItemFlags | Per-caller item gift flags |

unstructured_notes: Key QA scenarios: (1) Call cannot trigger when player is on entrance tile — test at every door tile. (2) Swarm reports must override wild encounter tables until midnight and then revert. (3) Prof. Oak only appears in contact list after Radio Card is obtained. (4) Item gift callers must not re-offer items until the daily flag resets. (5) The wrong-number behavior on empty slot should display gracefully, not crash. (6) Test that Fly destinations on Map card only show for visited locations and that Silver Cave appears as a Johto fly point.

goal: Implement radio system — 11 stations, Pokemon March/Lullaby (encounter rate mod), Oak's Pokemon Talk, Buena's Password, Team Rocket signal
abstract_tasks: Implement radio dial UI state machine (RADIOINIT/RADIOJOYPAD) integrating with Pokegear; map tuning to station by frequency/channel index; play station's assigned music track

Implement all 11 radio channels: Oak's Pokemon Talk (MUSIC_POKEMON_TALK), Pokedex Show (MUSIC_POKEMON_CENTER), Pokemon Music (MUSIC_TITLE), Lucky Channel (MUSIC_GAME_CORNER), Buena's Password (MUSIC_BUENAS_PASSWORD), Places and People (MUSIC_VIRIDIAN_CITY), Let's All Sing (MUSIC_BICYCLE), Team Rocket Radio (MUSIC_ROCKET_OVERTURE), PokeFlute Radio (MUSIC_POKE_FLUTE_CHANNEL), Unown Radio (MUSIC_RUINS_OF_ALPH_RADIO), Evolution Radio (MUSIC_LAKE_OF_RAGE_ROCKET_RADIO)

Implement Radio Card gating (Johto stations) and EXPN Card gating (PokeFlute, Kanto-accessible content); Unown Radio active only inside Ruins of Alph; Team Rocket Radio forced during Radio Tower takeover via RADIO_TOWER_MUSIC flag override

Implement Pokemon Music channel EXPN Card enhancement: play MUSIC_POKEMON_MARCH during day (increases wild encounter rate) and MUSIC_POKEMON_LULLABY at night (decreases wild encounter rate) when EXPN Card is held
Implement Lucky Channel lottery: weekly draw on Fridays of a random 5-digit number; match 2-5 trailing digits of any owned Pokemon's OT ID for prizes (PP Up/Exp.Share/Max Revive/Master Ball)

Implement Buena's Password: available 6PM-midnight (night only); pick password from 11 categories x 3 options with associated point values (6-16 pts); player visits Radio Tower 2F to redeem with Buena; Blue Card point exchange for items (Ultra Ball=2, Full Restore=2, Nugget=3, Rare Candy=3, Protein/Iron/Carbos/Calcium/HP Up=5)
Implement PokeFlute Radio station wake-up mechanic: standing next to Snorlax on Route 11 while tuned to PokeFlute channel triggers the battle encounter

source_data.radio_system.md:
## Radio Stations
### 1. Oak's Pokemon Talk — MUSIC_POKEMON_TALK; Routes discussed: 29,46,30,32,34,35,37,38,39,42,43,44,45,36,31
### 3. Pokemon Music — MUSIC_TITLE; EXPN Card adds Pokemon March (day, +encounter rate) and Lullaby (night, -encounter rate)
### 4. Lucky Channel — Weekly lottery Friday; match trailing digits of OT ID: 2=PP Up, 3=Exp.Share, 4=Max Revive, 5=Master Ball
### 5. Buena's Password — 6PM-midnight; 11 categories; Points: Johto Starters=10, Beverages=12, Healing Items=12, Balls=12, Pokemon 1=10, Pokemon 2=10, Johto Towns=16, Types=6, Moves=12, X Items=12, Radio Stations=13
Blue Card Prize Exchange: Ultra Ball=2, Full Restore=2, Nugget=3, Rare Candy=3, Protein/Iron/Carbos/Calcium/HP Up=5
### 8. Team Rocket Radio — MUSIC_ROCKET_OVERTURE; replaces all stations during Radio Tower takeover; RADIO_TOWER_MUSIC flag override
### 9. PokeFlute Radio — Kanto/EXPN Card required; MUSIC_POKE_FLUTE_CHANNEL; wakes Snorlax on Route 11
### 10. Unown Radio — Only inside Ruins of Alph; MUSIC_RUINS_OF_ALPH_RADIO
### 11 channels total (NUM_RADIO_CHANNELS = 11); exit restores map music (RESTART_MAP_MUSIC = 0xFE)

unstructured_notes: The radio replaces overworld map music while open. On exit, RESTART_MAP_MUSIC ($FE) is written to resume map music. When Team Rocket occupies the Radio Tower, all normal stations are replaced by the Rocket broadcast -- this is controlled by a special bit flag set on all Radio Tower floor maps. The encounter rate modification from Pokemon March/Lullaby applies to all wild encounter checks while the radio is playing in the overworld. PokeFlute waking Snorlax requires: player adjacent to Snorlax tile, currently tuned to PokeFlute channel, then the interaction triggers the battle. The Lucky Channel lottery number is generated once per Friday (stored), not per-draw. Buena's Password only airs at night (6PM-midnight) and the password category/option are randomized each day.

goal: Implement Pokedex system — seen/caught tracking, search modes (type, name, habitat), Oak's rating system, area display
abstract_tasks: Implement Pokedex state machine with 14 states: DEXSTATE_MAIN_SCR, UPDATE_MAIN_SCR, DEX_ENTRY_SCR, UPDATE_DEX_ENTRY_SCR, REINIT_DEX_ENTRY_SCR, SEARCH_SCR, UPDATE_SEARCH_SCR, OPTION_SCR, UPDATE_OPTION_SCR, SEARCH_RESULTS_SCR, UPDATE_SEARCH_RESULTS_SCR, UNOWN_MODE, UPDATE_UNOWN_MODE, EXIT

Implement three listing modes: New Pokedex Order (Johto dex numbering per dex_order_new.asm), Old Pokedex Order (National/Kanto-first 1-251 per dex_order_alpha.asm), Alphabetical Order (A-Z); persist current mode as wCurDexMode/wLastDexMode

Implement Pokedex entry screen: seen = silhouette sprite + species name + 'Seen' indicator; caught = full-color sprite + species name + category + height (ft/in) + weight (lbs) + dex text + cry playback; trigger NewPokedexEntry flow on first catch
Implement seen/caught bit-flag arrays for all 251 species (SetSeenAndCaughtMon, CheckCaughtMon, CheckSeenMon); derive total seen/caught counts displayed on trainer card and main dex screen

Implement search/filter by type (using data/types/search_types.asm and search_strings.asm) and alphabetical sort; display scrollable filtered results with count; only show species that have been seen or caught
Implement area display: mark Town Map with encounter type icons (grass/water/cave) for each map where species appears in encounter tables; use landmark system for location name display
Implement Professor Oak's PC rating tiers: accessible from any Pokemon Center PC; multiple threshold messages based on caught count; congratulatory message for all 251 caught
Implement cursor position memory: wPrevDexEntry stores last viewed entry, wDexListingScrollOffset stores scroll position; 7 entries visible at a time with smooth scrolling

source_data.pokedex_system.md:
## Pokedex Modes
| State | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| DEXSTATE_MAIN_SCR | Main listing screen |
| DEXSTATE_DEX_ENTRY_SCR | Individual entry view |
| DEXSTATE_SEARCH_SCR | Search/filter screen |
| DEXSTATE_UNOWN_MODE | Unown catalog |
| DEXSTATE_EXIT | Exit Pokedex |

### Listing Modes
New Pokedex Order (dex_order_new.asm), Old Pokedex Order (1-251 national), Alphabetical Order (dex_order_alpha.asm)
Stored in wCurDexMode, persists via wLastDexMode

### Entry Display
Seen but not caught: Species name, Silhouette sprite, 'Seen' indicator
Caught: Species name, Full-color sprite, Species category, Height (ft/in), Weight (lbs), Pokedex description text, Cry playback option

### Search Options
- By Type: data/types/search_types.asm, search_strings.asm
- Alphabetical: Sort A-Z

### Pokedex Flags
SetSeenAndCaughtMon, CheckCaughtMon, CheckSeenMon
251 species stored as bit arrays

### Area Display
Shows map locations where a species appears; markers for Grass/Water/Cave; uses landmark system (data/maps/landmarks.asm)

### Professor Oak's PC
Multiple threshold messages based on caught count; congratulatory for all 251

### Navigation
wPrevDexEntry remembers last viewed, wDexListingScrollOffset remembers scroll; 7 entries at a time

unstructured_notes: Dex entries are stored one file per species in data/pokemon/dex_entries/ with pointers in dex_entry_pointers.asm. Each entry has species name, height, weight, and description text. The cry playback button on the caught entry screen plays the species cry. The area display draws markers on a map overlay -- Grass marker, Water marker, Cave marker. The Unown Mode is a separate state (DEXSTATE_UNOWN_MODE) unlocked separately (see sprint 209). Professor Oak's PC is accessed from Pokemon Center PC terminals and evaluates the caught count with multiple tiers of commentary. The search result list still respects the seen/caught filter -- species never encountered are not shown even if they match the type filter.

goal: QA audit of sprints 202-203 — verify all radio stations, encounter rate modifiers from radio, Pokedex search accuracy, Oak's completion ratings

Verify Pokemon March increases and Pokemon Lullaby decreases wild encounter rate while playing; confirm EXPN Card required; confirm effect is active during overworld walking, not just in radio UI

Verify Lucky Channel lottery: number generated once per Friday; PP Up for 2-digit match, Exp.Share for 3, Max Revive for 4, Master Ball for all 5; test with Pokemon holding OT IDs with known trailing digits

Verify Buena's Password: only available 6PM-midnight; 11 categories with correct point values; Blue Card prize exchange costs match pokecrystal data; Buena's contact registered after first participation
Verify PokeFlute Radio wakes Snorlax on Route 11 when player is adjacent and tuned to correct channel; verify channel does not work in Johto before EXPN Card
Verify Pokedex listing modes: New/Old/Alphabetical all order species correctly; mode persists between Pokedex sessions; cursor position and scroll offset restored on re-entry
Verify Pokedex search by type returns only species with that type that have been seen/caught; alphabetical search orders correctly; area display shows correct map markers per species encounter table

Verify Professor Oak's PC rating messages trigger at correct caught-count thresholds; verify congratulatory message for 251 caught; verify seen vs caught counts displayed correctly on trainer card and main screen

### Listing Modes — New Order, Old Order, Alphabetical; wCurDexMode/wLastDexMode persist
### Search Options — By Type (search_types.asm), Alphabetical; only shows seen/caught species
### Area Display — Grass/Water/Cave markers; landmark names

unstructured_notes: Regression checklist: (1) Radio music must stop and map music resume on Pokegear close regardless of which card was last active. (2) Team Rocket Radio override must prevent all normal station access until the Radio Tower event is cleared. (3) Unown Radio must only be tunable inside Ruins of Alph maps -- verify it cannot be selected outside. (4) Pokedex should not show species in search results if never seen, even if type matches. (5) Oak's rating tiers should not all fire at once -- only the appropriate tier message.

goal: Implement save system — save data structure, save/load/new game, box change saves, SRAM integrity check, continue screen
abstract_tasks: Implement primary and backup dual-save structure: both slots written on every save; each slot has validation marker, player data, Pokemon data, current box, checksum; CheckSumFailure falls back to backup slot on load

Implement _SaveGameData 10-step save pipeline: set wSaveFileExists, StageRTCTimeForSave, BackupMysteryGift, ValidateSave, SaveOptions, SavePlayerData, SavePokemonData, SaveBox, SaveChecksum, then backup copy, then UpdateStackTop, BackupPartyMonMail, BackupGSBallFlag, SaveRTC, clear BattleTower reward state

Implement save data scope: player name/ID/gender/position/direction/money/coins/badges; party Pokemon; PC box metadata (14 boxes x 20 mons each, only current box in WRAM); Pokedex seen/caught flags; options; event flags/variables; RTC state; Mystery Gift; Hall of Fame; link battle stats; Battle Tower state
Implement box-change save (ChangeBoxSaveGame): triggered on PC box switch; show 'SAVING... DON'T TURN OFF THE POWER' message; save current box to SRAM, load new box from SRAM

Implement new game flow: ErasePreviousSave pipeline (EraseBoxes all 14, EraseHallOfFame, EraseLinkBattleStats, EraseMysteryGift, SaveData, EraseBattleTowerStatus, clear stack top); gender selection (Crystal exclusive: boy/girl choice via init_gender.asm setting wPlayerGender)

Implement save file detection and overwrite protection on boot: AskOverwriteSaveFile checks player ID match; different-player-ID shows warning; same-player-ID prompts overwrite; no save proceeds silently; delete-save accessible via Up+Select+B on title screen

Implement continue screen: display save file info -- player name, badges, Pokedex caught count, play time; Hall of Fame entry system (NUM_HOF_TEAMS max, FIFO eviction, Virtual Console GS Ball hook after first HOF entry)

source_data.menu_systems.md:
### Save Process (_SaveGameData)
1. Set wSaveFileExists = TRUE
2. StageRTCTimeForSave
3. BackupMysteryGift
4. Write primary save: ValidateSave, SaveOptions, SavePlayerData, SavePokemonData, SaveBox, SaveChecksum
5. Write backup save: identical structure with Backup prefix
6. UpdateStackTop, BackupPartyMonMail, BackupGSBallFlag, SaveRTC, Clear BattleTower reward

### Save Corruption Protection
- Dual save slots
- Checksums per slot
- Validation markers
- CompareLoadedAndSavedPlayerID

### Box Change Save
ChangeBoxSaveGame: 'SAVING... DON'T TURN OFF THE POWER'; save current box, load new box

### What Gets Saved
Player data: name, ID, gender, position, direction, money, coins, badges
Pokemon data: party, PC box metadata, Pokedex flags
Current box contents (only one box in WRAM at a time)
Options, event flags, RTC, Mystery Gift, Hall of Fame, link battle stats, Battle Tower state

### Overwrite Protection
1. No save: proceed (erase remnants)
2. Same player ID: 'Already a save file. Is it OK to overwrite?'
3. Different player ID: 'Another save file...' warning

### Hall of Fame Save
NUM_HOF_TEAMS max entries; new entry shifts old entries down; Virtual Console GS Ball hook after first HOF

### Erase Previous Save
EraseBoxes, EraseHallOfFame, EraseLinkBattleStats, EraseMysteryGift, SaveData, EraseBattleTowerStatus

### Gender Selection
Crystal exclusive: boy/girl, sets wPlayerGender

unstructured_notes: The save system is critical infrastructure. A corruption here is the worst possible bug. The dual-save slot system means we always have a backup. The checksum validation must be bit-exact with pokecrystal's implementation. Box-change saves are partial saves -- only the box contents change, not the full save. SRAM layout must match memory_map.md exactly for compatibility. The Virtual Console GS Ball hook fires after the first Hall of Fame entry, enabling the GS Ball event that was otherwise unobtainable. Player ID is a 5-digit number generated on new game; this is how the game distinguishes save files. Maximum character lengths: player name=7, nickname=10, box name=8.

goal: Implement NPC dialogue system — key NPC text, move tutors (3 tutors: Headbutt, Flamethrower/Thunderbolt/Ice Beam, Elemental punches), name rater
abstract_tasks: Implement move tutor 1 (Ilex Forest): teaches Headbutt to any compatible Pokemon; one-time or repeatable interaction; check TM/HM compatibility list for Headbutt (HM equivalent teaching)

Implement move tutor 2 (Goldenrod Game Corner prize room / Celadon alternate): teaches Flamethrower, Thunderbolt, or Ice Beam for coins; player selects which move; teach to selected party Pokemon if compatible and has open move slot or chooses to replace
Implement move tutor 3 (Blackthorn City Move Tutor house): teaches Fire Punch, Ice Punch, or ThunderPunch once per move per game; check species compatibility; permanent one-time flags per move taught

Implement Name Rater (Lavender Town): assess current nickname of selected party Pokemon; offer to rename if player accepts; open naming screen with current nickname prefilled; rename is permanent (nickname flag updated)

Implement key NPC text for: Prof. Elm (lab dialogue, story calls), Guide Gent (gives Map card, Cherrygrove tutorial), Bill (PC box full, first meeting), Prof. Oak (Radio Card dialogue, Pokedex encouragement), Move Deleter (Blackthorn City, removes any move including HMs), Clefairy (National Park??)

Implement scripting_commands.md text command pipeline for NPC dialogue: text speed from wOptions, player name insertion via buffer, line break handling, text box dimensions and scroll, waitbutton/waitbuttonorspace
Implement in-battle and overworld item tutor NPC text strings per game_text_strings.md canonical list; ensure text IDs match pokecrystal for potential replay accuracy

source_data.menu_systems.md:
### Character Limits
| Context | Max Length |
|---------|----------|
| Player name | 7 characters |
| Pokemon nickname | 10 characters |
| Box name | 8 characters |

### Options Menu -- Text Speed
- Fast: TEXT_DELAY_FAST (1 frame per character)
- Medium: TEXT_DELAY_MED (3 frames per character)
- Slow: TEXT_DELAY_SLOW (5 frames per character)
- Stored in low nibble of wOptions

unstructured_notes: The three move tutors in Crystal are: (1) Headbutt tutor in Ilex Forest who teaches it for free repeatedly. (2) The Game Corner tutor who teaches Flamethrower/Thunderbolt/Ice Beam for coins (this may actually be in Celadon or Goldenrod depending on version -- cross-check pokecrystal johto_map_scripts.md). (3) The Blackthorn move tutor who teaches elemental punches once each per game. Move Deleter in Blackthorn removes any move including HMs (unlike Gen 3+ where HMs cant be deleted without the Deleter). Name Rater is in Lavender Town -- he rates the nickname and offers to change it. The text system must handle player name substitution (the @PLAYER@ placeholder becomes the actual player name in all dialogue).

goal: QA audit of sprints 205-206 — verify save/load integrity, continue screen data, move tutor availability, NPC dialogue accuracy
abstract_tasks: Verify dual-save integrity: corrupt primary save slot checksum and confirm backup is loaded correctly; verify checksum algorithm is bit-exact with pokecrystal behavior
Verify box-change save: switch PC boxes and confirm partial save occurs; simulate power cut during box switch and verify no data loss on next load
Verify new game overwrite protection: same player ID prompts 'overwrite?', different player ID shows warning; Up+Select+B on title screen accesses delete-save correctly
Verify continue screen displays correct player name, badge count (badges from wBadges flags), Pokedex caught count, and play time from save file without loading full game state

Verify Hall of Fame: first completion creates HOF entry; subsequent completions add new entries up to NUM_HOF_TEAMS limit; oldest entry evicted when full; GS Ball flag set after first HOF entry (Virtual Console hook)

Verify all three move tutors: Headbutt tutor teaches correctly and respects compatibility; Game Corner tutor requires correct coin amount and teaches correct moves; Blackthorn tutor one-time flags prevent re-teaching same move
Verify Move Deleter removes any move including HMs; confirm party Pokemon's move slot is cleared, PP data cleared, and move replaced with empty; verify party is not left in invalid state
Verify Name Rater flow: opens naming screen with current nickname prefilled; successful rename updates nickname; canceling leaves nickname unchanged; nickname flag (OT vs player) updated correctly

source_data.menu_systems.md:
### Save Corruption Protection
- Dual save slots (primary + backup)
- Checksums per slot
- Validation markers (ValidateSave/ValidateBackupSave)
- CompareLoadedAndSavedPlayerID

### Box Change Save
ChangeBoxSaveGame triggered on PC box switch
'SAVING... DON'T TURN OFF THE POWER' message

### Overwrite Protection
AskOverwriteSaveFile: same ID = overwrite prompt, different ID = warning, no save = proceed

### Hall of Fame
NUM_HOF_TEAMS max; FIFO eviction; Virtual Console GS Ball hook after first entry

### Continue Screen
Displays: player name, badges, Pokedex count, play time

### Erase Previous Save
EraseBoxes (all 14), EraseHallOfFame, EraseLinkBattleStats, EraseMysteryGift, SaveData, EraseBattleTowerStatus

unstructured_notes: Critical save integrity tests: (1) Write garbage bytes to primary save checksum area, verify backup loads. (2) Start box switch, simulate abort mid-write, verify no corruption. (3) Create a save with player ID 12345, start new game, verify the different-ID warning triggers. Move tutor audit: verify Headbutt is actually teachable to correct species per the TM/HM compatibility data. Verify that Blackthorn tutor flags persist through save/load cycles. NPC dialogue audit: check that player name substitution works in all contexts where @PLAYER@ appears.

goal: Implement trainer card — badges display, money, Pokedex count, play time, ID number
abstract_tasks: Implement trainer card screen layout: player name and ID number prominently displayed; money amount; Pokedex seen and caught counts; play time in hours:minutes (max display 999:59); badges visual display

Implement badges display: read wBadges byte (8 badge bits for Johto) and wKantoBadges byte (8 Kanto badge bits); render badge icons in correct grid positions matching pokecrystal trainer_card.asm layout
Implement play time tracking: increment wPlayTimeHours/wPlayTimeMinutes/wPlayTimeFrames per frame during gameplay; cap display at 999:59; persist play time in save data
Implement trainer ID display: 5-digit ID number (wPlayerID, 2 bytes, displayed as decimal 00000-65535); ID generated randomly on new game
Implement trainer card gender variant: Crystal has Chris (male) and Kris (female) trainer cards with different background art based on wPlayerGender selection at new game start
Implement start menu player name entry point: selecting player name on start menu opens trainer card screen (not a separate name entry -- the trainer card IS the player status screen)
Implement money display on trainer card: read wMoney (3-byte BCD value); display with currency symbol; cap at 999999 (pokecrystal MAX_MONEY)

source_data.menu_systems.md:
## Trainer Card (trainer_card.asm)
### Displayed Information
- Player name and ID number
- Money
- Pokedex: Seen / Caught counts
- Badges: visual display of collected badges
- Play time
source_data.game_constants.md:
### Level Limits
- MAX_LEVEL = 100
- MAX_STAT_VALUE = 999

### Battle Types (wBattleType)
| Value | Constant | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| 0 | BATTLETYPE_NORMAL | Standard battle |
| 12 | BATTLETYPE_SUICUNE | Suicune encounter |

unstructured_notes: The trainer card in Crystal has two visual variants for Chris (male) and Kris (female) protagonist. The card accesses wBadges (Johto) and wKantoBadges (Kanto) -- each is a byte where bits 0-7 correspond to the 8 gym badges in order. Badge icons must match the correct gym leader's badge in the correct grid position. Play time is tracked in frames per minute, not via RTC -- it accumulates even when the RTC is not functioning correctly. The 999:59 cap is a display limit; internal tracking can go higher. Money is stored as BCD (binary-coded decimal) in 3 bytes allowing values 0-999999. The trainer card is accessed from the start menu by selecting the player's name (5th option in standard start menu).

goal: Implement Unown Pokedex mode — Unown form collection (26 letters), Ruins of Alph puzzle unlock tracking, special Unown word display
abstract_tasks: Implement Unown Pokedex mode (DEXSTATE_UNOWN_MODE / UPDATE_UNOWN_MODE): 26-cell display of Unown letter forms (A-Z); each cell shows form's unique sprite glyph if caught, blank if uncaught

Implement Unown mode unlock condition: STATUSFLAGS_UNOWN_DEX_F set in wStatusFlags after catching at least 3 different Unown forms; store unlock state in wUnlockedUnownMode; Unown Mode option only appears in Pokedex OPTION_SCR when unlocked

Implement Unown form unlock tiers via puzzle completion: Kabuto puzzle unlocks forms A-K (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_A_TO_K); Omanyte puzzle unlocks L-R (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_L_TO_R); Aerodactyl puzzle unlocks S-W (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_S_TO_W); Ho-Oh puzzle unlocks X-Z (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_X_TO_Z)

Implement Unown word display per form (from data/pokemon/unown_words.asm): A=ANGRY, B=BEAR, C=CHASE, D=DIRECT, E=ENGAGE, F=FIND, G=GIVE, H=HELP, I=INCREASE, J=JOIN, K=KEEP, L=LAUGH, M=MAKE, N=NUZZLE, O=OBSERVE, P=PERFORM, Q=QUICKEN, R=REASSURE, S=SEARCH, T=TELL, U=UNITE, V=VANISH, W=WARN, X=XXXX, Y=YEILD, Z=ZOOM

Implement Ruins of Alph chamber secret trigger system: Kabuto chamber uses Escape Rope in-chamber; Omanyte chamber requires Water Stone in bag; Aerodactyl chamber requires using Flash in-chamber; Ho-Oh chamber requires Ho-Oh in party; each trigger opens hidden wall to item room and sets corresponding unlock flag

Implement Wild Unown encounter availability: after puzzle completion, Inner Chamber (main ruins hall) spawns wild Unown matching unlocked letter set; each Unown form has its own sprite loaded from data/pokemon/unown_pic_pointers.asm

source_data.ruins_of_alph.md:
## Puzzle Chambers
### 1. Kabuto Chamber
Wall Hint: ESCAPE; Secret: Use Escape Rope in chamber; Unlocks: Unown A through K (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_A_TO_K); Hidden Items: Berry, PsnCureBerry, Heal Powder, EnergyPowder
### 2. Omanyte Chamber
Wall Hint: WATER; Secret: Have a Water Stone in bag; Unlocks: Unown L through R (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_L_TO_R); Hidden Items: MysteryBerry, Mystic Water, Stardust, Star Piece
### 3. Aerodactyl Chamber
Wall Hint: LIGHT; Secret: Use Flash in chamber; Unlocks: Unown S through W (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_S_TO_W); Hidden Items: Gold Berry, Moon Stone, Heal Powder, Energy Root
### 4. Ho-Oh Chamber
Wall Hint: HO OH; Secret: Have Ho-Oh in party; Unlocks: Unown X through Z (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_X_TO_Z); Hidden Items: Gold Berry, MysteryBerry, Revival Herb, Charcoal

source_data.pokedex_system.md:
## Unown Mode (unown_dex.asm)
### Unlock Condition
- STATUSFLAGS_UNOWN_DEX_F must be set in wStatusFlags
- Unlocked by catching specific Unown forms
- Stored in wUnlockedUnownMode

### Display
- Shows all 26 Unown forms (A-Z)
- Each form displayed as its unique letter glyph
- Only caught forms shown (uncaught forms blank)

### Unown Data
- data/pokemon/unown_pic_pointers.asm: sprite pointers for each form
- data/pokemon/unown_words.asm: associated words for each letter

unstructured_notes: The Unown mode threshold is 3 different forms caught before the mode unlocks. The unlock is not tied to puzzle completion -- it's tied to actually catching 3 Unown. However, Unown only spawn after puzzle completion, so solving at least the Kabuto puzzle (unlocking A-K) is a prerequisite. Each puzzle's secret trigger is a one-time in-chamber action: the chamber must detect the specific action (Escape Rope use, Flash use, item presence, party member presence) and respond by opening the secret passage. The hidden wall becoming passable should be permanent (flagged). The word display in Unown Mode shows the word associated with each letter -- these are lore tidbits. Note that U=UNITE, V=VANISH, W=WARN, X=XXXX (placeholder in data), Y=YIELD, Z=ZOOM -- verify exact spellings from pokecrystal unown_words.asm.

goal: QA audit of sprints 208-209 — verify trainer card display, badge tracking, Unown form registration, Ruins of Alph Pokedex mode
abstract_tasks: Verify trainer card displays correct player name, ID (5-digit decimal), money (BCD, capped at 999999), Pokedex seen/caught counts, play time (hours:minutes, capped at 999:59), and all 16 badge positions (8 Johto + 8 Kanto)
Verify badge bit tracking: each gym victory sets the correct bit in wBadges (Johto) or wKantoBadges (Kanto); badge icons on trainer card correspond to the correct gym leader for each bit position
Verify Crystal gender variant: male player (Chris) shows male trainer card art, female player (Kris) shows female trainer card art; gender persists through save/load
Verify Unown Pokedex mode unlock: mode option does not appear before catching 3 different Unown forms; appears after STATUSFLAGS_UNOWN_DEX_F is set; mode persists through save/load
Verify Unown form unlocking by puzzle: Kabuto solved -> A-K encounter in Inner Chamber; Omanyte solved -> L-R; Aerodactyl solved -> S-W; Ho-Oh solved -> X-Z; forms from locked puzzles do not spawn

Verify all four Ruins of Alph chamber secret triggers: Escape Rope used in Kabuto chamber opens passage; Water Stone in bag opens Omanyte chamber; Flash used in Aerodactyl chamber opens passage; Ho-Oh in party opens Ho-Oh chamber; each trigger is permanent (re-entering does not require re-triggering)
Verify Unown Mode display: caught forms show their letter glyph sprite; uncaught forms show blank; correct word displayed for each form (A=ANGRY through Z=ZOOM per unown_words.asm)
Verify that Unown form sprites load correctly for all 26 forms from unown_pic_pointers.asm; no sprite corruption or incorrect form displayed for any letter

## Trainer Card
Displayed Information: player name, ID number, money, Pokedex Seen/Caught, badges, play time

unstructured_notes: Badge regression checklist: earn each of the 16 badges and verify the correct icon lights up. Particularly important: the 8 Kanto badges (Brock/Boulder, Misty/Cascade, Surge/Thunder, Erika/Rainbow, Janine/Soul, Sabrina/Marsh, Blaine/Volcano, Blue/Earth) must be tracked separately from Johto badges in wKantoBadges. Unown mode should be accessible from Pokedex OPTION_SCR only after unlock. Ruins puzzle trigger verification: test each chamber's secret action both before and after the puzzle is solved to confirm the trigger logic is tied to puzzle solve state.

goal: Implement slot machine — 3 reels (15 symbols each), payout table, bias/mode system, coin purchase, prize redemption
abstract_tasks: Implement 3-reel slot machine state machine: player presses A to stop each reel sequentially; reel positions drawn from exact 15-symbol composition per reel (Reel1: Seven/Cherry/Staryu/Pikachu/Squirtle/Seven/Cherry/Staryu/Pikachu/Squirtle/Pokeball/Cherry/Staryu/Pikachu/Squirtle; Reel2: Seven/Pikachu/Cherry/Squirtle/Staryu/Pokeball/Pikachu/Cherry/Squirtle/Staryu/Pokeball/Pikachu/Cherry/Squirtle/Staryu; Reel3: Seven/Pikachu/Cherry/Squirtle/Staryu/Pikachu/Cherry/Squirtle/Staryu/Pikachu/Pokeball/Cherry/Squirtle/Staryu/Pikachu)
Implement bet system: 1/2/3 coins per spin; 1 coin = middle row only; 2 coins = middle + top + bottom rows; 3 coins = all 5 lines including both diagonals

Implement payout table: Seven-Seven-Seven=300 coins, Pokeball-Pokeball-Pokeball=50, Cherry-Cherry-Cherry=6, Pikachu-Pikachu-Pikachu=8, Squirtle-Squirtle-Squirtle=10, Staryu-Staryu-Staryu=15; payout multiplied by bet

Implement bias/mode system: on game init, 12.5% chance machine is 'lucky' (wKeepSevenBiasChance=TRUE), else normal; each spin generates a bias symbol via threshold table (Normal: ~1%=Seven, ~1%=Pokeball, ~4%=Staryu, ~8%=Squirtle, ~16%=Pikachu, ~19%=Cherry, ~81%=no bias; Lucky: approximately doubled Cherry bias ~31%); when bias active, reel manipulation searches up to 4 extra positions to land the biased symbol

Implement special Seven streak mechanics: when bet>=2 and Reel1 shows Seven, 31.25% chance Reel2 fast-spins to line up its Seven; if both reels show Seven and bias=Seven: 29.7% normal stop / 23.4% slow advance / 23.4% Golem (shakes machine) / 23.4% Chansey (walks across, drops egg, advances reel 3 by 17 positions); non-Seven-bias path: no Chansey mode; after Seven jackpot, Normal machines keep Seven bias with 25% chance, Lucky machines keep with 12.5%

Implement coin purchase and prize redemption: player buys coins at Game Corner desk; coin cap = 9999 (wCoins/COIN_CASE_MAX); prize exchange desk accepts coins for prizes (TM prizes, Pokemon prizes per Game Corner prize data)
Implement known slot machine payout sound bug: sound effects cut each other off during coin dispensing (faithful recreation as documented bug)

source_data.mini_games.md:
## Slot Machine
### Reel 1 (15 symbols): Seven/Cherry/Staryu/Pikachu/Squirtle x3 + Pokeball at pos 10
### Reel 2 (15 symbols): Seven at 0, Pokeball at 5 and 10, rest Pikachu/Cherry/Squirtle/Staryu
### Reel 3 (15 symbols): Seven at 0, Pokeball at 10, rest Pikachu/Cherry/Squirtle/Staryu
### Payout Table: Seven-Seven-Seven=300, Pokeball-Pokeball-Pokeball=50, Cherry=6, Pikachu=8, Squirtle=10, Staryu=15
### Lines: 1 coin=middle, 2 coins=middle+top+bottom, 3 coins=all 5
### Bias System
Normal Machine: ~1%=Seven, ~1%=Pokeball, ~4%=Staryu, ~8%=Squirtle, ~16%=Pikachu, ~19%=Cherry, ~81%=no bias
Lucky Machine (12.5% of machines): ~1%=Seven, ~31%=Cherry, ~69%=no bias
Manipulation: up to 4 extra positions per reel to land bias symbol
### Seven Streak
Both reels show Seven -> Reel3 modes (when biased=Seven): 29.7% normal / 23.4% slow / 23.4% Golem / 23.4% Chansey
After jackpot: Normal=25% keep bias, Lucky=12.5% keep bias (apparently inverted -- possible developer bug)
### Known Bug: payout SFX cut each other off during dispensing

unstructured_notes: The slot machine is the primary coin-farming method. The bias system makes it feel 'fair' -- the machine nudges reels toward the bias symbol. Golem mode: Golem drops down and shakes the machine, advancing reel 3 by a variable slot count. Chansey mode: Chansey walks across and drops an egg, advancing reel 3 by 17 positions repeatedly until Sevens line up -- this guarantees the jackpot when Chansey triggers and bias=Seven. The Lucky vs Normal mode determination comment in pokecrystal code says the behavior seems inverted (Lucky has worse streak odds) -- this may be a developer bug; we faithfully replicate the original behavior. Coin cap is 9999; COIN_CASE_MAX must be enforced at purchase and payout. The prize redemption desk is separate from the slot machine and must track coin balance deduction.
